message: Jake
Günter
I am
visiting the doctor's office.
In the office, there was a reception desk at the entrance, and the receptionist looked at me, puzzled, and asked.
Receptionist
Excuse me, but who are you?
I’m a friend of Kristy Darn,
a Snipes.
Receptionist
A friend of Kristy Darn? That means you're not a patient, right?
Yes. However, I came to ask about her condition as her friend.
Receptionist
Even so, I can't share other people's privacy matters with a stranger like you.
Receptionist
Plus the doctor has a tight schedule.
I completely understand that. However, since her fiancé is also my friend and he asked me to check on her,
I spent about 10 minutes persuading the receptionist and, as a result, secured an appointment with Doctor Günter.
You are
Snipes, right?
Yes. Thank you for making time for me in your busy schedule.
Hmm, I want you to remember that I am squeezing time out while keeping patients waiting.
Oh, it's you again. What a nuisance.

About Kristy

to another place

exit the game
The office was a perfectly ordinary, common room.
This room, which eliminated all unnecessary items, felt somewhat lacking in warmth and gave me a chilling impression.
Even though it wasn't particularly artificial or isolating, why does it feel so emotionally distant?
Just being in this room makes me feel uneasy.
I had imagined someone more mild-mannered, so it's hard to believe that the man before me
is a doctor.
His nervous vibe and sharp gaze make it impossible for me to confide in this person.
He has that condescending attitude typical of doctors and lawyers.
I can't say I like him much.
I should clarify from the start that I want you to understand I’m taking this time specially for you.
I appreciate it.
You might have heard, but despite being just one person, patients keep coming to me.
So, I want you to keep in mind that this is valuable time.
I often wish for a day to have 72 hours.
Do you understand? That's how busy I am.
Hah.
I don't want to waste any time on unnecessary talk. Keeping that in mind, please ask your questions.
Just know that there are patients waiting for me who need my attention.
Miss Dern is a little mentally disturbed.
Still, I think it's only mild.
I have to say that sounds like a layman's assessment.
It's certainly a layman's assessment, but.
Even if the appearance shows no disabilities, it’s quite common for the mind to be heavily troubled.
One thing to be cautious about is that Miss Dern seems to have originally suffered some kind of psychological trauma.
Is that so!?
In modern society, it wouldn't be an exaggeration to say that almost everyone lives with some kind of psychological trauma.
However, that's not such a big deal, and as long as counseling continues, it's definitely something that can be healed.
Kristy Dern is already my patient. I’m convinced that as long as she continues with counseling, she will surely get better.
I appreciate your understanding.
But, there’s the guy who is her fiancé, right? As long as he stays by Miss Dern’s side, things should progress quickly.
I don’t think there’s anything left to say to you.
Now is not my interview time.
That’s true, but...
According to the receptionist, you had questions regarding Miss Kristy Dern.
I don’t want to waste my precious time on needless talk. If you have nothing more to say, then just leave already.
I wonder if it’s okay to leave it to the doctor.
Even if he is a psychiatrist recommended by Yuriko, I can’t possibly think this doctor will make things better through counseling.
But, the current situation isn’t particularly bad for Kristy, and counseling may indeed be having its effect.
I know he’s busy. But what’s with this cold attitude?
It must be incredibly annoying for him to take time for me when he doesn’t even have time to spare as I’m not even a patient.
I wish he could at least show a hint of a smile.
Well then, I’ll take my leave. Thank you very much.
I would like you to never bother me again.
